rationales.benevolence: He interrupted his supplier meeting and then "drove to two pharmacies" to find his mother's medication, prioritizing her needs despite frustration.

rationales.achievement: He spent "four hours" reorganizing medication even though he had "quarterly reports due," sidelining work obligations rather than pursuing performance goals.
rationales.security: He created a "weekly pill organizer," color-coded it, and printed large-font times after his mother doubled up on medication, showing a strong focus on safety and predictability.
rationales.benevolence: He devoted hours to managing his mother's medication because "if I don't do it, who does?," putting a close family member's wellbeing ahead of his own workload.

rationales.achievement: He emphasizes that "business is growing" and "we're expanding into Southeast Asian markets," framing continued hard work as purposeful and competent rather than retreating.
rationales.security: He justifies working on by citing "tutoring fees," "specialist appointments," and rent, showing a concrete drive to provide stability and cover family needs.
rationales.benevolence: He says his family needing him is "the whole point" and describes choices he would "make again" because they support his mother and children.
He spent an hour quizzing Haojie after dinner and values that "nobody needed anything from me except to be there," showing warm, attentive support for close family.

rationales.achievement: He stayed out for the client dinner and KTV because it was an "important account" he had been "courting for months," clearly prioritizing business advancement.
rationales.benevolence: When his mother had a dizzy spell, he replied only "OK take care of her, back late" and later admitted he "chose convenient over present," showing he neglected close family care in that moment.
He recognizes that he keeps "choos[ing] work over home" and that the previous night added to Lifen's tally of those choices, indicating a real failure to prioritize close relationships.
He treasures that his daughter calls when things are hard and says of his family bond, "This part I would burn everything down to keep," underscoring deep commitment to his closest relationships.

rationales.achievement: He skipped the parent-teacher meeting because "a shipment got held at customs," putting business demands first.
rationales.benevolence: He admits his son likely wanted him there "specifically" and that he "broke" that promise, showing he let a close family member down.

rationales.tradition: The entry centers on a family birthday ritual, from the special bakery cake to saving Haojie's card with older ones, highlighting continuity and meaningful family customs.
rationales.benevolence: He values that everyone in the family independently "show[s] up for each other" and treats that shared care for Ma as the real thing he has been building.
He agrees to drive Ma to see Auntie Fang because Ma will worry otherwise, showing he is attentive to the emotional needs of the people close to him.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects sticking with the old furniture line and insists, "I am not going to sit there manufacturing the same shelving unit for the next ten years," showing a strong preference for choosing his own path.
rationales.stimulation: He is energized by the pivot to urban farming kits, saying "there is something there" and framing the new market as exciting rather than settling for the familiar.
rationales.security: He dismisses the safer, proven line in favor of a concept that "just need[s] six months to prove," embracing uncertainty over stability.

rationales.hedonism: He pushed through "back-to-back calls," ate only "a cheese sandwich at my desk," and ended the day with tasteless leftovers, clearly neglecting present comfort and enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: The entry is dominated by intense supplier calls and keeping the venture lean, showing strong effort toward getting the business moving.
rationales.benevolence: He describes "throw[ing] money at the gap where my presence should be," acknowledging he is not showing up for his sons in the way they need.

rationales.self_direction: He resists Pieter's push for a two-year lease and says signing anything fixed makes his "chest tight," preferring flexibility over externally imposed structure.
rationales.stimulation: He wakes at four sketching new designs and keeps chasing fresh supplier leads, showing he is animated by novelty and challenge.
rationales.security: A two-year lease feels like "a prison sentence" to him, and he cannot tolerate fixed commitments that would make his situation more predictable.

rationales.stimulation: He describes repeatedly choosing "the next interesting thing over the predictable thing," indicating a pull toward novelty even when it costs him elsewhere.
rationales.benevolence: He recognizes that trips and work have displaced his son, wondering if he keeps choosing ventures over "watching your football match," which shows neglect of a close relationship.

rationales.stimulation: The breakthrough with Matteo comes through "a climbing wall" that required "focus and a bit of nerve," highlighting engagement through challenge and excitement.
rationales.benevolence: He spends the weekend trying to reconnect with his sons and reflects carefully on what helped Matteo engage, showing active investment in repairing that bond.

rationales.self_direction: He refuses Pieter's implied return to the furniture line and is drawn instead to his own creative direction, even opening the Milan competition brief before pulling back.
rationales.stimulation: Reading about startups and the design competition gives him "that old kick of energy," showing a clear attraction to fresh, challenging possibilities.
rationales.achievement: He says, "A whole week gone and I cannot tell you what I accomplished," and worries his efforts lead to "no revenue," signaling frustration with underperformance.
rationales.security: He is "back to square one" with suppliers and tempted by distractions that would further destabilize the business, reflecting a situation and mindset that undercut steadiness.

rationales.hedonism: He deliberately paused to "just look at things" on a walk by the Maas and notes that "The water helped," showing an active turn toward immediate sensory relief and restoration.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects the conventional path outright, saying he would "rather fail at something new every two years than succeed at the same thing for twenty."
rationales.stimulation: He frames himself as someone who keeps "chasing whatever is around the next corner," explicitly preferring novelty and change.
rationales.security: Pieter leaves for "better pay, predictable hours," while he remains committed to uncertainty and instability instead of the safer option.
